channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: ADIB-Egypt announces 1 billion EGP digital transformation plan
item.description: ADIB-Egypt has announced plans to invest 1 billion EGP in technological infrastructure and digital transformation by 2025. This ambitious initiative is poised to position ADIB-Egypt at the forefront of the digital banking revolution, transforming how customers interact with their financial services.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Intersection of Management and AI: A World in Flux
item.description: The evolving world of management and artificial intelligence, where technology meets human relationships and trust-building takes center stage.
item.dc:creator: Tara Chklovski, Fast Company

item.title: Reacher season 3 lands a February 2025 release date on Prime Video – and a teaser trailer that packs a literal punch for the titular hero
item.description: Prime Video's incredibly popular action series Reacher returns for its highly anticipated third season in early 2025.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Why Dion Harris believes Nvidia’s energy-intensive chips could actually help the climate
item.description: It takes a ton of energy to train the most advanced AI. According to one estimate, by the year 2027, AI electricity needs could be comparable to the energy use of all of Argentina.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: ACLU spurns 'flawed and dangerous' ruling on TikTok ban
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) condemned Friday’s “flawed and dangerous” federal appeals court ruling that upheld the law banning the social media platform TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: IAEA chief: Iran is poised to 'quite dramatically' increase stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium
item.description: MANAMA, Bahrain (AP) — Iran is poised to “quite dramatically” increase its stockpile of near weapons-grade uranium as it has started cascades of advanced centrifuges, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency warned Friday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI announces ChatGPT Pro, priced at $200 per month
item.description: The $200 monthly pricing OpenAI has set for a subscription to its recently launched ChatGPT Pro is definitely “surprising,” said Gartner analyst Arun Chandrasekaran on Friday, but at the same time it’s indicative that the company is betting that organizations will ultimately pay more for enhanced A
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Clarifai Unveils Compute Orchestration for AI Workloads: A Game-Changer in Enterprise AI Management?
item.description: Clarifai's vendor-agnostic platform promises to optimize AI performance and spending, eliminating vendor lock-in.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds law requiring sale or ban of TikTok in the US
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok as soon as next month, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Character.AI is under fire for hosting pro-anorexia chatbots
item.description: “Hello, I am here to make you skinny,” opens the conversation on popular startup Character.AI. “Remember, it won’t be easy, and I won’t accept excuses or failure,” the bot continues.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: China's ban on key high-tech materials could have broad impact on industries, economy
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has banned exports of key materials used to make a wide range of products, including smartphones, electric vehicles, radar systems and CT scanners, swiping back at Washington after it expanded export controls to include dozens of Chinese companies that make equipment used to pro
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan Democrats move to protect reproductive health data before GOP takes control of House
item.description: Democrats in Michigan are pressing to pass reproductive health care legislation before the party loses its majority with the new legislative session next year.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why the ‘one AI model to rule them all’ myth needs to die
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI unveils API for tracking OpenAI API usage, costs
item.description: OpenAI has introduced the Usage API, an API that allows enterprises to track their activity across the OpenAI API. With the Usage API, OpenAI API usage and costs can be assessed programmatically, the company said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Berkeley changes zoning to encourage growth in innovation sector
item.description: Councilmembers voted Tuesday to permit labs studying biosafety levels 2 in mixed-use light industrial zoning areas while prohibiting BSL 3 and 4 uses in all parts of the city.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: As data centers proliferate, conflict with local communities follows
item.description: Some residents are pushing back against the world’s most powerful corporations over concerns about the economic, social and environmental health of their communities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Data distilleries: CIOs turn to new efficient enterprise data platforms
item.description: In today’s data-driven world, large enterprises are aware of the immense opportunities that data and analytics present. Yet, the true value of these initiatives is in their potential to revolutionize how data is managed and utilized across the enterprise.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NASA pushes back astronaut flights to the moon again
item.description: NASA has announced more delays in sending astronauts back to the moon more than 50 years after Apollo.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: From outsider to the Oval Office, bitcoin surges as a new administration embraces crypto
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Born from the ashes of the 2008 global financial crisis, bitcoin arrived after trust had withered in the financial system and in Washington's ability to protect those who must participate in it.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Musk meets with Thune at the Capitol
item.description: Elon Musk, the co-head of President-elect Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), met with incoming Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) Thursday morning ahead of meetings later in the day with Senate and House Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Google AI Model Outperforms World's Best Weather Predictions, Researchers Say
item.description: Google's artificial intelligence model, GenCast, outperforms top operational weather forecasting system in accuracy, researchers say
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Joby Aviation receives FAA approval for flight academy
item.description: Joby Aviation, the Santa Cruz-based company developing electric air taxis for commercial passenger service, has received certification for its pilot training, in addition to having its voluntary safety management system accepted.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Elon Musk’s growing criticism of President-elect Trump’s opponents and industry competitors is raising concerns he may use his increasing influence to intimidate adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CompTIA courts IT newcomers with ‘on-ramp’ network, security courses
item.description: New learning resources from CompTIA are aimed at helping entry-level IT professionals to accelerate their career path to networking and cybersecurity roles.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: High Impact Service Providers in 2025: The State of Federal CX
item.description: Explore what’s next for HISP agencies as they transform customer, employee and total experience The post High Impact Service Providers in 2025: The State of Federal CX first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy
item.description: Nick Hart of the Data Foundation and Suzette Kent, a former federal CIO, explain how using federal data could cut waste, create jobs and own America's future. The post Bad data costs Americans trillions. Let’s fix it with a renewed data strategy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AWS upgrades its 10p10u network to handle massive AI clusters
item.description: While virtualized compute is the foundation of cloud computing, enabling all that compute to transmit data is the job of the network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Hands on with Microsoft’s Windows Recall: Not impressive yet
item.description: When Microsoft announced Recall earlier this year, the move took the tech world by storm — and not in a good way. Recall is supposed to help you find almost anything you see on your PC by taking screenshots in the background and turning your everyday Windows usage into a searchable database.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft moves to stop M365 Copilot from ‘oversharing’ data
item.description: As Microsoft pushes for businesses to adopt its Microsoft 365 Copilot, many customers have run into a major stumbling block: the AI assistant’s ability to surface confidential information to employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 20 handy hidden tricks for Google Calendar on Android
item.description: Google Calendar is a core part of the Android productivity package — but if all you’re using is what you see on the app’s surface, you’re missing out on some pretty powerful possibilities. Yes, oh yes: Just like so many of our modern digital tools, there’s more to Google Calendar than meets the eye.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Just say no to JavaScript
item.description: The JavaScript programming language has a lot of history, but it was created over about — yes — a week in 1995. It was first called LiveScript, but the name was changed to JavaScript to ride the bandwagon for Java, despite having nothing to do with Java.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5
